text: C:/ my documents/ resus scenarios/ assessment tool medical students/ July 09 NEONATAL RESUSCITATION SKILL FLOW SHEET Assessor: • Place a tick (����) in the relevant box, depending upon whether criteria has been met or not met. Participant may ask questions about the baby’s condition at any stage. Identify how you would prepare for resuscitation of a newborn. CRITERIA C NYC 1 PREPARATION 1.1 Checks & tests as appropriate - risk factors � all equipment � safety clothing - gloves & goggles available � danger (obstruction / wet floor etc) � Baby with apnoeic and cyanosed. 2 INITIAL STEPS 2.1 Notes time of birth / time baby found 2.2 Assesses baby’s general condition – Term / preterm gestation � clear amniotic fluid (meconium/infection) � breathing / crying � muscle tone � 2.3 Dries baby 2.4 Removes wet linen, provides warmth 2.5* If meconium present and baby non responsive, indicates need for suctioning under direct vision before stimulation. Keep warm. When clear proceed to resuscitation Baby remains apnoeic 3 AIRWAY 3.1 Places baby on preheated warmer with firm flat surface 3.2 Positions head in neutral position or “sniffing the air” 3.3 Applies gentle tactile stimulation, particularly thorax, flick soles of feet 3.4 Applies oropharyngeal suctioning if indicated - mouth then nose 3.5 Assesses: respiratory effort � heart rate � colour � non responsive cyanosis � after 30 seconds (for a period of 6 secs) Baby remains apnoeic, heart rate 9 beats in 6 seconds (90bpm), cyanosed 4 BREATHING 4.1 Raises the alarm - not breathing � gasping � HR <100bpm � non responsive cyanosis � 3.5 Administers free flowing oxygen as necessary (if breathing but persistently cyanosed) 4.2 Inadequate respiratory effort or not breathing and / or heart rate < 100 bpm - commences bag & mask � or Neopuff ventilation � 4.3 Correct technique - mask size � hand position � mask seal � head tilt � inflation � O2 flow � 4.4 Performs manual ventilations at a rate of 40-60 bpm (~1 – 1.5/sec) 4.5 Confirms adequate chest movement and response 4.6 Reassesses: respiratory effort � heart rate � colour � after 30 seconds (for a period of 6 secs) Baby remains apnoeic, heart rate 6 beats in 6 seconds (60bpm) and slowing, cyanosed 5 CIRCULATION 5.1 If heart rate < 60 bpm - Commences CPR 6 ONE OPERATOR CPR or TWO OPERATOR CPR (verbalise) 6.1 Ratio: 3 compressions to 1 breath (4 events in 2 secs) with hesitation of ECC to deliver breath Demonstrate techniques 2 thumbs/two finger 6.2 Rate: 120 events / min (90 compressions & 30 ventilations) 6.3 Correct – position � depth � vertical compression � 6.4 Good ‘Two Operator’ team work: pause in compressions � effective ventilation � counting out loud � communication � 6.5 Reassesses: respiratory effort � heart rate � colour � after 60 seconds (for a period of 6 secs) Two possible outcomes 1) No response or 2) Baby responds C:/ my documents/ resus scenarios/ assessment tool medical students/ July 09 Heart rate remains less than 6 beats in 6 seconds (60bpm) 7 NO RESPONSE TO EFFECTIVE CPR and VENTILATION 7.1 Identifies likely causes � reviews technique � 7.2 Indicates the need for endotracheal intubation and drug administration 7.3 Identifies equipment required for endotracheal intubation 7.4 Identifies ‘first line’ drug that may be required (Adrenaline – strength � dose � route � Baby shows signs of recovery AS ASSESSOR DETERMINES– eg heart rate increasing, breathing, tone improving, colour improving 8 RECOVERY 8.1 Gradually withdraws interventions as baby recovers as indicated by condition a) ETT b) CPR c) Ventilations d) oxygen 8.2 Maintains airway 8.3 Considers oro-gastric tube 8.4 Provides care as indicated by degree of resuscitation routine care � observational care � post resuscitation care � 8.5 Monitors - airway � breathing � circulation � 8.6 Provide warmth - Wraps baby warmly � or skin to skin contact � 8.7 Gives baby to mother when condition satisfactory 8.8 Ensures Doctor reviews baby as soon as possible 8.9 Completes relevant documentation 8.10 Identification bands applied. 8.11 Notify SCN of pending admission OVERALL ASSESSMENT Strengths: Areas for development: Assessor_________________________________________ C:/ my documents/ resus scenarios/ assessment tool medical students/ July 09
